objective: Define a shared D0-visible enriched security state / tradability metadata contract for future clean baseline and challenger research inputs only.

visibility_rules.trade_date_alias_policy: trade_date is an optional warehouse alias only and must equal signal_date whenever present.

fail_fast_rules: fail fast if any field cannot be proven D0 visible
fail fast if any field uses D1 or later execution outcome
fail fast if any field uses frozen test evidence
fail fast if trade_date and signal_date disagree
fail fast if point-in-time effective dates are missing for stateful fields
fail fast if source_snapshot_id is missing or not bound to the declared research snapshot
fail fast if d0_visible is false
fail fast if no_frozen_test_access is false

fields.d0_visibility: Snapshot binding is known at build time and must refer to the declared D0-visible research snapshot.
fields.leakage_risk: Rebinding after reviewing later evidence would corrupt provenance and make downstream state fields unauditable.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Missing snapshot binding or snapshot binding differs from the declared research snapshot.

fields.d0_visibility: Instrument identifier is known on D0 and used as part of the canonical primary key.
fields.leakage_risk: Using a later remapped identifier or post-delist symbol rewrite can backfill future identity state.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Instrument identifier is missing, duplicated within primary key, or cannot be matched to security master.

fields.d0_visibility: Optional warehouse alias only; if present it must equal signal_date and represent the same D0 session.
fields.leakage_risk: If trade_date drifts from signal_date, later-session state can be mistakenly injected.
fields.fail_fast_condition: trade_date is present but not equal to signal_date.

fields.d0_visibility: Canonical D0 date field; all state fields must be interpretable as of this date only.
fields.source: research snapshot row date / canonical trading session date
fields.leakage_risk: A non-canonical signal_date can shift the interpretation window and silently import future state.
fields.fail_fast_condition: signal_date is missing, non-trading-session aligned, or inconsistent with the declared snapshot date semantics.

fields.d0_visibility: Must reflect whether the security is under ST or equivalent risk-warning status on D0.
fields.source: point-in-time security status table / exchange risk-warning state table
fields.leakage_risk: Using current-name ST detection or backfilled status tables can leak future risk warnings into history.
fields.fail_fast_condition: ST state cannot be resolved from a point-in-time source for D0.

fields.d0_visibility: Must identify the D0-valid upstream ST source or precedence rule used to derive is_st.
fields.leakage_risk: Opaque derivation hides whether the ST flag came from a future-corrected source.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Source precedence for ST resolution is missing or ambiguous.

fields.d0_visibility: Must be the historical effective start date already known by D0 for the active ST interval.
fields.leakage_risk: Backfilled interval starts can make the status appear known earlier than it was.
fields.fail_fast_condition: is_st=true but st_effective_start is missing or after signal_date.

fields.d0_visibility: Must be the interval end only if already recorded by D0; open intervals remain null.
fields.leakage_risk: Using the eventual end date before it becomes known would leak future regime resolution.
fields.fail_fast_condition: st_effective_end is populated from a future-known closeout date not available on D0.

fields.d0_visibility: Must reflect D0 suspension status only, based on the D0 trading session state.
fields.source: exchange suspension state table / trading status snapshot
fields.leakage_risk: Using D1 resumption or later execution outcome to infer D0 suspension would leak future tradability.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Suspension status cannot be resolved or conflicts irreconcilably with D0 market-state source.

fields.d0_visibility: Must indicate whether D0 ended with no valid trade prints for the session.
fields.source: daily market data state from D0 volume/amount/trade count and trading status
fields.leakage_risk: Inferring from later fills or portfolio outcome would turn execution results into input state.
fields.fail_fast_condition: No-trade rule is undefined or cannot distinguish suspension from ordinary zero-print sessions.

fields.d0_visibility: Must be derived only from D0 session volume field.
fields.source: daily volume field in D0 market data snapshot
fields.leakage_risk: A repaired or forward-filled volume field can hide true D0 no-trade state.
fields.fail_fast_condition: D0 volume field is missing, non-numeric, or sourced from a repaired future-aware table.

fields.d0_visibility: Must be derived only from D0 session amount/turnover field.
fields.source: daily amount field in D0 market data snapshot
fields.leakage_risk: Using a later-adjusted amount field can overwrite true D0 trading absence.
fields.fail_fast_condition: D0 amount field is missing, non-numeric, or inconsistent with the no_trade_flag rule.

fields.d0_visibility: Must reflect whether D0 session ends in an upper-limit state under the D0-valid price-limit regime.
fields.source: D0 market data plus board-aware limit rule lookup
fields.leakage_risk: Using D1 open-board behavior or a future regime table would leak tradability resolution.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Upper-limit status cannot be reproduced from D0 prices and the declared limit_rule_version.

fields.d0_visibility: Must reflect whether D0 session ends in a lower-limit state under the D0-valid price-limit regime.
fields.source: D0 market data plus board-aware limit rule lookup
fields.leakage_risk: Using later recovery or reopen information would inject future downside resolution.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Lower-limit status cannot be reproduced from D0 prices and the declared limit_rule_version.

fields.d0_visibility: Must indicate whether the D0 open price is already at the upper limit under the D0-valid rule.
fields.source: D0 open price plus board-aware limit rule lookup
fields.leakage_risk: Post-open intraday behavior must not be used to redefine the opening limit state.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Open price or upper-limit rule inputs are missing.

fields.d0_visibility: Must indicate whether the D0 close price equals the D0 lower limit.
fields.source: D0 close price plus board-aware limit rule lookup
fields.leakage_risk: Using next-day close or later adjudicated limit records would leak future session state.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Close price or lower-limit rule inputs are missing.

fields.d0_visibility: Must identify the price-limit regime version that is already valid on D0 for the given board and exchange.
fields.leakage_risk: A retroactively normalized rule version can overwrite historical limit semantics.
fields.fail_fast_condition: limit_rule_version cannot be explained by board_type, exchange, and limit_pct_rule on D0.

fields.d0_visibility: Must be a D0-only buy-side eligibility state derived from D0 market-state blockers, not from D1 fill results.
fields.source: contracted combination of D0 suspension, limit, quote, and no-trade blockers
fields.leakage_risk: Using entry_filled_D1 or real execution success would convert outcomes into features.
fields.fail_fast_condition: entry_buyable depends on any D1+ execution field or an unspecified blocker precedence.

fields.d0_visibility: Must be a D0-only sell-side state derived from D0 market-state blockers and planned exit semantics known by D0.
fields.leakage_risk: Using future exit path resolution would silently import realized execution information.
fields.fail_fast_condition: exit_sellable depends on any D1+ realized exit or unresolved future repair outcome.

fields.d0_visibility: Must represent a policy-state flag declared from D0-observed blockers only; it cannot rely on whether the next open actually traded.
fields.source: tradability policy contract over D0 blockers
fields.leakage_risk: Looking at the next open outcome to define retry eligibility would leak future execution availability.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Retry state is inferred from next-day realized trading instead of D0 blocker taxonomy.

fields.d0_visibility: Must be the listing date already known by D0 under the chosen point-in-time security master.
fields.leakage_risk: Later corrected listing dates or merged identities can backfill future knowledge into historical age.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Listing date is missing, duplicated with conflicting values, or not point-in-time auditable.

fields.d0_visibility: Must be computed from list_date to signal_date using calendar-day difference only.
fields.leakage_risk: Manual overrides or future-aware recoding of list_date would distort age buckets.
fields.fail_fast_condition: listing_age_days cannot be reproduced from list_date and signal_date exactly.

fields.d0_visibility: Must be computed from list_date through the D0 trading calendar only.
fields.source: derived from list_date and the declared exchange trading calendar through D0
fields.leakage_risk: Using a later-repaired trading calendar can make the age count unavailable at the original time.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Trading-day calendar binding is missing or the count cannot be reproduced through D0.

fields.d0_visibility: Must be a deterministic thresholded state derived from D0-visible listing_age fields only.
fields.source: derived from list_date, listing_age_days, and/or listing_age_trading_days using fixed threshold policy
fields.leakage_risk: A threshold tuned on later performance would turn the flag into a disguised alpha filter.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Threshold policy is missing, mutable, or depends on future performance slices.

fields.d0_visibility: Must identify the D0-valid board or segment assignment for the security.
fields.source: point-in-time security master / board assignment table
fields.leakage_risk: Applying today's board label to history can rewrite the applicable trading regime.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Board assignment lacks D0 point-in-time evidence or conflicts with exchange segment source.

fields.d0_visibility: Must identify the D0-valid exchange for the security.
fields.leakage_risk: Identifier remapping after later corporate events can backfill exchange identity.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Exchange code is missing or conflicts with instrument identity binding.

fields.d0_visibility: Must encode the D0-valid percentage-limit regime implied by board_type and exchange.
fields.leakage_risk: Using a later normalized regime label can misprice historical limit states.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Percentage-limit regime is absent or inconsistent with the declared board_type or exchange.

fields.d0_visibility: Must record the exact upstream D0-visible snapshot binding used to build the row.
fields.leakage_risk: Without immutable snapshot provenance, future rebinding can masquerade as the original state build.
fields.fail_fast_condition: source_snapshot_id is missing or differs from declared snapshot binding rules.

fields.d0_visibility: Audit-only timestamp of when the artifact was built; not a market-state input.
fields.leakage_risk: Missing build metadata makes it impossible to audit when a state artifact was materialized.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Artifact build timestamp is missing or not serialized in a stable timestamp format.

fields.d0_visibility: Audit-only builder identifier describing the contract implementation version.
fields.leakage_risk: Unknown builder version makes downstream reproducibility and diff auditing impossible.
fields.fail_fast_condition: Builder version is missing or not pinned to a governed contract release.

fields.d0_visibility: Audit assertion flag and must be true for every emitted row.
fields.leakage_risk: A false or omitted flag means downstream consumers cannot trust the entire artifact boundary.
fields.fail_fast_condition: d0_visible is false, null, or omitted.

fields.d0_visibility: Audit assertion flag and must be true for every emitted row and build attempt.
fields.leakage_risk: If frozen test access is allowed or ambiguous, the contract boundary is invalidated.
fields.fail_fast_condition: no_frozen_test_access is false, null, omitted, or contradicted by run provenance.
